_comment: Spike T2.0 P0 exit artifact: complete field inventory of both pilot state formats with per-field classification, plus the CMS-SNES v1 correspondence and transforms. Every byte-level claim cites path:line in the pinned vendored trees under tools/transmute/vendor/ — format truth is the cited source, never this file or memory. Direction of the pilot codec: DECODE Mesen2 .mss -> CMS; ENCODE CMS -> bsnes stream via power-on donor template (spec H6).

pins.citation_convention: tree:path:line[@range] where tree is mesen2|bsnes, path is repo-relative

layout.note: u32 LE; load gate: must be <= running emulator version (SaveStateManager.cpp:169-172)
u32 LE = 4 (SaveStateManager.h:23); load gate: >= 3
u32 LE; Snes = 0 (SettingTypes.h:358-367); load gate: must match running console (or GB-family compat path)
u32 LE framebuffer byte count (SaveVideoData, SaveStateManager.cpp:115-129)
u32 LE; load caps 2 MB (GetVideoData)
zlib-wrapped framebuffer; SKIP for decode purposes (emulator-internal)

display filename; NOT validated on load (H7 REVISED: no ROM identity check in format v4; SHA1 existed only <= v3, SaveStateManager.cpp:180-200). Identity discipline is OURS: transmutation requires a sidecar manifest sha256 match
Emulator::Serialize output, framing below

mesen2_payload_framing.cite: mesen2:Utilities/Serializer.cpp:64-146,207-230; Core/Shared/Emulator.cpp (Serialize; includeSettings=false for .mss states via SaveStateManager::SaveState:78-82)

layout.note: 1 for .mss (compressionLevel default 1, Emulator.h:187)
u32 LE; load cap 10 MB (Serializer.cpp:85-88); ABSENT when is_compressed=0 (raw records to EOF)
u32 LE; same cap; absent when uncompressed
zlib (compress2) of the record stream

record_format.record: [key: bytes in (0x20,0x7F) exclusive][0x00][u32 LE value_size][value bytes]
record_format.key_rules: dot-joined prefixes; leading '_' and 'state.' stripped, leading uppercase run per segment lowercased, '[i]' suffix for indexed (Serializer.cpp:237-290)
record_format.load_semantics: whole stream indexed into a key->value map; unknown keys tolerated silently; missing keys leave target field unchanged (Serializer.h:271-283). Multi-byte values LE (WriteValue, Serializer.h:104-113)

layout.note: u32 LE byte length of the RLE<1> section
u32 LE; 0 when no preview captured (states.cpp:84-91)
nall Encode::RLE<1> of the serializer stream
RLE<2> of 256x240 RGB555 preview; emulator-internal, skip

rle_codec.format: u64 LE decoded-size header, then packets: control<0x80 => (control+1) literal words follow; control>=0x80 => one word repeated ((control&0x7F)+M) times

layout.note: exact total stream size; load gate: must equal per-cartridge dry-run size (serialization.cpp:41,103-119)

layout.value: '115.1' NUL-padded; load gate: exact string match (emulator/emulator.hpp:38)

layout.note: free text, not validated
bool; UI/runner saves are always 1 (interface.hpp:61); =0 is the rewind variant carrying raw cothread stacks (serialization.cpp:90-97) — REFUSE on decode, never emit
bool; load gate: must match running configuration.hacks.ppu.fast (default TRUE, sfc/interface/configuration.hpp:38). Selects the PPU block layout below

bsnes_stream_header.load_behavior: unserialize: 4 gates only (signature, exact size, version string, fastPPU flag) — no checksum, no ROM identity, no semantic validation; synchronize=1 causes power(reset=false) BEFORE the positional read (serialization.cpp:25-48). H5 CONFIRMED; H6 donor strategy rests on this power-cycle.

bsnes_stream_blocks._comment: serializeAll order (bsnes:bsnes/sfc/system/serialization.cpp:52-98). Plain LoROM/HiROM cart => NO coprocessor blocks; controllerPort1/2 + expansionPort serialize ZERO bytes (sfc/controller/controller.cpp:60-61, sfc/expansion/expansion.cpp:36-37). Integer encoding: LE at sizeof(underlying type); nall Natural<N>/Integer<N> serialize at their utype width 1/2/4/8 (nall/serializer.hpp:85-125, nall/primitives/natural.hpp:8-13); bool and nall boolean = 1 byte.
bsnes_stream_blocks.coprocessor_blocks_refused: ICD MCC DIP Event SA1 SuperFX ARMDSP HitachiDSP NECDSP EpsonRTC SharpRTC SPC7110 SDD1 OBC1 MSU1 Cx4 DSP1 DSP2 DSP4 ST0010 BSMemory SufamiTurboA/B — presence keyed off cartridge.has.* (serialization.cpp:60-84); our encoder targets plain carts only, and cart RAM size mismatch fails the exact-size gate anyway

fields.synthesis: donor (PCG state; only affects future power-on randomization, no gameplay effect post-boot)

cpu.cite: bsnes:bsnes/sfc/cpu/serialization.cpp:1-107; widths from sfc/cpu/cpu.hpp:83-260, processor/wdc65816/wdc65816.hpp:25-289, sfc/sfc.hpp:77-125, sfc/ppu/counter/serialization.cpp

fields.synthesis: constant 0 (WDC65816 zero register, an emulator implementation artifact)

fields.transform: unpack NVMXDIZC byte to 8 bool bytes in stream order c,z,i,d,x,m,v,n

fields.synthesis: donor (last-taken interrupt vector address; consumed only mid-interrupt-dispatch, impossible at instruction-boundary capture)
donor (mid-instruction temporaries; dead at instruction boundary)
donor + timing-domain alignment rule: relative scheduler offset; P2 sets from donor then verifies live-injection equivalence (control C4 isolates any error to semantics vs format)

fields.rule: one-instruction IRQ suppression after specific ops; quiescent captures: false
per-scanline refresh slot (~538); synthesize from timing domain; both models agree at frame-edge captures
0/1/2 state machine; synthesize: 0 if hclock<position else 2 (refresh done)
derived OR of pending lines; recompute from truth table outputs
33=inactive (cpu.hpp:118); quiescent captures park outside auto-read window => 33
manual controller strobe line ($4016 bit0); architectural-real: copy from Mesen2 controlManager strobe

fields.transform: unpack $4200: bit4->hirq, bit5->virq (bsnes irqEnable is h&v combined semantics: hirq|virq stored separately + irqEnable — MAP: hirqEnable=bit4, virqEnable=bit5, irqEnable=0 donor-checked in P2, nmiEnable=bit7, autoJoypadPoll=bit0)
bsnes stores (dot+1)<<2 master-clock comparator (sfc/cpu/io.cpp:204-215); CMS stores raw dot; encode: (cms+1)<<2

fields.rule: in-flight multiply countdown; Mesen2 internalRegisters.aluMulDiv.multCounter maps directly (same iterative model); quiescent: 0

fields.transform: per-channel 21 bytes in serialization.cpp:88-107 order: dmaEnable(1) hdmaEnable(1) direction(1) indirect(1) unused(1) reverseTransfer(1) fixedTransfer(1) transferMode(1) targetAddress(1) sourceAddress(2) sourceBank(1) transferSize(2) indirectBank(1) hdmaAddress(2) lineCounter(1) unknown(1) hdmaCompleted(1) hdmaDoTransfer(1); $43x0 DMAP bit-mapping: direction=bit7, indirect=bit6, unused=bit5, reverseTransfer=bit4, fixedTransfer=bit3, transferMode=bits0-2
unpack NVPBHIZC byte to bool bytes in stream order c,z,i,h,b,p,v,n

fields.synthesis: donor + P2 alignment (CPU<->SMP relative phase; H4 primary hazard — StateProbe v2 measures tolerance)
donor (SMP->DSP tick divider; pairs with dsp.clock)

fields.transform: per-timer 8 bytes: stage0(1) stage1(1) stage2(1) stage3(1,u4) line(1) enable(1) target(1)... NOTE stream order per smp/serialization.cpp:31-53 is stage0,stage1,stage2,stage3,line,enable,target = 7 fields/timer; stage3 = 4-bit output counter (read-to-clear $FD-FF -> CMS apu.timer[n].output); Mesen2 equivalents: timerN.stage0/prevStage1/stage1(?)/output/target/enabled/timersEnabled — exact stage-name correspondence pinned in P2 against both step functions; both model the same divider chain

ppu_fastppu_true.cite: bsnes:bsnes/sfc/ppu/serialization.cpp:1-8 (display prefix + dispatch) + sfc/ppu-fast/serialization.cpp:1-166; widths sfc/ppu-fast/ppu.hpp:10-290
ppu_fastppu_true._comment: DEFAULT and encode-target layout (hacks.ppu.fast=true). Stream = 3 display ints, then ppufast fields.

fields.synthesis: same as cpu.ppucounter: vcounter/hcounter/field from timing domain, periods recomputed
fields.note: PPU-side counter runs at its own thread position; P2 pins whether donor parks both counters equal (runToSave semantics suggest yes at sync point)

fields.synthesis: recompute from io fields (per-frame render latches)

fields.note: fast PPU keeps reload in oamBaseAddress and live address here; accurate PPU splits differently — P2 pins the exact reload/live correspondence against write handlers

WindowLayer.mask is uint (4 bytes) — sfc/ppu-fast/ppu.hpp:144; bg window enables map from W12SEL/W34SEL bits; per-bg above/below from TM/TS; tileMode recomputed from bgMode by encoder (updateVideoMode donor duplicate)

fields.cms: ppu.wobjsel + ppu.obsel + ppu.tm_ts + ppu.range_time_over + ppu.oamaddr_reload(first sprite)

fields.path: ppufast.io.col (window 4xbool+mask(4)+aboveMask(4)+belowMask(4) + enable[7] + directColor + blendMode + halve + mathMode + fixedColor(2))
fields.note: fixedColor is one u16 BGR; CMS coldata b/g/r 5-bit components pack into it

fields.transform: OAM raw 544 bytes <-> 128 decoded objects: low-table 4 bytes/sprite (x low 8, y, character, attr byte: vflip<<7|hflip<<6|priority<<4|palette<<1|nameselect) + high-table 2 bits/sprite (size, x bit8). Exact bit packing pinned against ppu-fast/io.cpp oamWrite/oamRead in P2 (byte-level oracle: StateProbe OAM seed)

ppu_fastppu_true.load_note: Line::start/count reset on load (ppu-fast/serialization.cpp:11-12) — renderer queue is never carried

ppu_fastppu_false._comment: Accurate-PPU layout, NOT the pilot encode target (default build runs fastPPU=true; our encoder emits fastPPU=true streams; the runner never produces accurate-layout states in the pipeline). Inventoried for completeness; bst_dump identifies it from the header flag and reports out-of-pilot-scope rather than mis-decoding.

field_groups.cms: ppu.vram_prefetch, oam/cgram write buffers, bgofs latches, m7 latch, counter latch state, oam/cgram internal addresses
ppu_regs domain (same register file as fast side, different layout/widths)

field_groups.path: ppu.mosaic + bg1..bg4 (io + output/mosaic/opt pipelines + 8-tile fetch queues + renderingIndex/pixelCounter, serialization.cpp:86-135)
field_groups.note: mid-scanline fetch/output pipelines: donor-synthesized; io fields map to CMS bg[4]

field_groups.path: ppu.obj (oam.object[128] decoded + io + latch.firstSprite + t.item/t.tile evaluation state + output pipes, serialization.cpp:137-193)

dsp.cite: bsnes:bsnes/sfc/dsp/serialization.cpp:1-28; blob layout sfc/dsp/SPC_DSP.cpp:949-1016 (copy_state), copier semantics SPC_DSP.cpp:905-946 + SPC_DSP.h:289-304

fields.synthesis: donor (DSP<->SMP tick phase; pairs with smp.io.dspCounter)

fields.class: architectural (514 meaningful bytes; zero-padded to state_size=640)
fields.transform: blargg copy_state order: regs[128]; per-voice x8: brr_buf[12]xi16, interp_pos u16, brr_addr u16, env u16, hidden_env i16, buf_pos u8, brr_offset u8, kon_delay u8, env_mode u8, t_envx_out u8, extra(1 zero byte); echo_hist[8][2]xi16; every_other_sample u8, kon u8, noise u16, counter u16, echo_offset u16, echo_length u16, phase u8, new_kon u8, endx_buf u8, envx_buf u8, outx_buf u8, t_pmon u8, t_non u8, t_eon u8, t_dir u8, t_koff u8, t_brr_next_addr u16, t_adsr0 u8, t_brr_header u8, t_brr_byte u8, t_srcn u8, t_esa u8, t_echo_enabled u8, t_main_out[2]xi16, t_echo_out[2]xi16, t_echo_in[2]xi16, t_dir_addr u16, t_pitch u16, t_output i16, t_echo_ptr u16, t_looped u8, extra(1 zero byte). All multi-byte LE (copy_int via SET_LE16).

mesen2_key_inventory._comment: Top-level domains from SnesConsole::Serialize (mesen2:Core/SNES/SnesConsole.cpp:87-100), streamed with empty console prefix (Emulator.cpp Serialize: s.Stream(_console, "")). Sizes are self-described per record; expected sizes stated for validation. Classification mirrors the bsnes table; 'cms' names the CMS field.

keys.rule: bitfield of asserted IRQ sources; truth table

keys.transform: enum SnesCpuStopState {0 Running,1 Stopped,2 WaitingForIrq} (SnesCpuTypes.h:5-10) -> stp=(v==1), wai=(v==2)

keys.cms: cpu.wai companion (WAI exit bookkeeping); quiescent: consistent with stopState

keys_architectural.rule: live vs reload CGRAM address split; pin against write handlers in P2

keys_architectural.note: RAW OAM bytes — decode side needs no object unpacking; the OAM bit-packing transform is encode-side only

keys_internal.synthesis: mosaicScanlineCounter -> CMS ppu.mosaic_vcounter (ambiguous_with_rule); draw window internal
mid-scanline fetch pipeline — not carried (frame-edge/vblank captures render nothing mid-line; bsnes fast PPU has no equivalent)

keys.transform: decrement<->reverseTransfer? NO: mesen2 'decrement'=A-addr step direction = bsnes 'fixedTransfer'? — naming crosswalk pinned by register bit, not name: $43x0 bit7 invertDirection=direction, bit6 hdmaIndirectAddressing=indirect, bit5 unusedControlFlag=unused, bit4 decrement=reverseTransfer, bit3 fixedTransfer=fixedTransfer, bits0-2 transferMode=transferMode; destAddress=targetAddress($43x1), srcAddress=sourceAddress, srcBank=sourceBank, transferSize=transferSize/indirectAddress, hdmaBank=indirectBank, hdmaTableAddress=hdmaAddress, hdmaLineCounterAndRepeat=lineCounter, unusedRegister=unknown($43xB), hdmaFinished=hdmaCompleted, doTransfer=hdmaDoTransfer
raw dot (InternalRegisters.cpp:352-360); CMS canonical = raw dot

keys.rule: maps to bsnes alu.divctr/mpyctr/shift; prevCpuCycle emulator-internal (not carried); quiescent: counters 0
progress + 16-bit port shift values; quiescent: inactive

keys.synthesis: IRQ comparison mirrors of timing domain; recompute

keys.note: presence of any key under these prefixes => REFUSE (chip firewall)

keys.synthesis: not carried (SPC master-cycle count; relative CPU<->SPC phase is the donor+P2 alignment problem)

keys.note: $F0 TEST decomposed; timersDisabled is bit0, timersEnabled bit3, ram bits per SpcState
output = 4-bit read-to-clear counter (bsnes stage3); stage0/1/2 divider chain; prevStage1 edge memory — P2 pins the stage-name crosswalk against both step() functions

keys.rule: SPC700 micro-step state machine. DECODE RULE: opStep MUST indicate instruction start; otherwise REFUSE (partial-instruction replay can double-consume read-to-clear registers). Mesen2's own Spc::Serialize runs Run() catch-up before saving (Spc.cpp:454-457) which lands wherever the CPU clock fell — StateProbe beacon captures land the SPC in its idle loop; P2 measures refuse-rate empirically

1-cycle-delayed CPU->APU port write staging. DECODE RULE: pendingCpuRegUpdate MUST be false (quiescent), else flush staged values into port_from_cpu (validity of the flush verified in P2 via live-injection C4)

keys.note: Mesen2 keeps a write-visible copy; bsnes has one file — encode uses regs; P2 pins which copy feeds $F3 reads and asserts both equal at quiescent capture else refuse

keys.transform: envVolume<->env, prevCalculatedEnv<->hidden_env, interpolationPos<->interp_pos, envMode<->env_mode (enum values pinned P2), brrAddress<->brr_addr, brrOffset<->brr_offset, keyOnDelay<->kon_delay, envOut<->t_envx_out, bufferPos<->buf_pos, sampleBuffer<->brr_buf; voiceBit recomputed (1<<n)

keys.note: 32-step sample cycle position <-> blob phase; unit crosswalk pinned P2 (both count DSP clocks/sample-pair)

keys.cms: dsp.pipeline_regs (t_pitch, t_brr_next_addr, t_dir_addr, t_brr_header, t_brr_byte, t_looped, t_adsr0, t_srcn)

keys.rule: controller shift register mid-serial-read. bsnes serializes NO controller state (empty ControllerPort::serialize) and re-latches from input on next strobe — a mid-manual-read capture loses shift position in bsnes REGARDLESS of our codec. Quiescent rule: capture outside manual serial reads (auto-read results live in joy1-4 which carry). Divergence risk bounded to one input poll; StateProbe does not exercise manual serial reads

interrupt_truth_table._comment: P0 correspondence sketch; P2 finalizes against both edge-detector implementations with StateProbe v3 cadence audits. Both model the same hardware: NMI asserted at vblank start when enabled; RDNMI bit7 sticky-until-read; IRQ on H/V timer match; lines held until acknowledged.

bsnes_fields: nmiTransition (edge seen, CPU not yet dispatched)
nmi.quiescent_rule: capture parked where no NMI edge is mid-pipeline: nmiFlag maps to nmiLine; needNmi=false maps to nmiTransition=nmiPending=false; nmiValid/nmiHold recomputed from timing domain. StateProbe v0 beacon sits mid-frame (no vblank edge in flight).

irq.quiescent_rule: no timer match mid-pipeline: irqFlag maps to irqLine and r.irq; transition/pending false; valid/hold recomputed.

decode_mss: format_version < 3 (v4 expected; v3 accepted per Mesen2's own gate after P2 verification of key deltas)
any key with prefix cart.coprocessor. / cart.bsxMemPack. / cart.gameboy. / msu1.
spc.opStep not at instruction start (spc_sub_instruction rule)
spc.pendingCpuRegUpdate true (unless P2 validates the flush rule)
manifest sha256 mismatch (OUR identity discipline; the format has none — H7)
encode_bst: target cart profile must be plain LoROM/HiROM with declared SRAM size matching sram.data length (bsnes exact-size gate enforces mechanically)
donor template REQUIRED from the same ROM + same bsnes build (H6 power-on donor)
decode_bst: fastppu=0 => out-of-pilot-scope report (accurate-PPU layout inventoried but not walked by bst_dump v1)
cartridge.has.* inference: stream size != plain-cart dry-run size for the ROM => coprocessor blocks present => refuse (mechanical chip firewall)

open_p2_pins: OAM raw<->decoded-object bit packing (both PPUs' oamWrite/oamRead) — oracle: StateProbe OAM seed
SPC timer stage-name crosswalk (SpcTimer<N>::step vs bsnes Timer<Frequency>::step)
Mesen2 externalRegs vs regs divergence semantics at capture
bsnes io.irqEnable composite semantics vs Mesen2 enableH/V (donor-check)
Thread.clock donor values: confirm runToSave parks all threads at clock offsets the donor reproduces (C4 isolates)
fast-PPU io.oamAddress/oamBaseAddress live-vs-reload split vs Mesen2 oamRamAddress/InternalOamAddress
